result:
I would collapse the distance between imagination and reality, so everything we can vividly think, dream, or need would be straightforwardly real—no laboratories, no supply chains, no compromises. Hunger ends because the image of a feast becomes the table already set; loneliness ends because the thought of companionship summons the person you long for; despair ends because a wish for possibility simply converts into next moment’s fact.

Most visions of progress still keep the gap intact: “better” technologies, sober plans, careful politics. Those steps matter, but they still make us wait and bargain. I’m interested in the gap itself. Remove it and every form of suffering that is merely logistical becomes illegitimate overnight—not eased, vanished.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
